title: galium mollugo subsp. corrudifolium
intro: Galium mollugo subsp. corrudifolium, also known as the bedstraw or cleavers, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, Galium mollugo subsp. corrudifolium is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions that can be used to treat a range of ailments.
